option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
REM Copy these commands one by one into Command Prompt
cd "C:\Projects\Personal Java Projects\microservice3"
git rm --cached docker-compose.yml git rm --cached inventoryservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached bookingservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached orderservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached apigateway\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached terraform.tfstate git rm -r --cached volume-data
del docker-compose.example.yml del SETUP-CREDENTIALS.md del PRE-PUSH-CHECKLIST.md del SECURITY-REVIEW.md del remove-sensitive-files.bat
git add . git commit -m "Security: Untrack sensitive files and add monitoring docs" git status

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# How to Run Your Microservices Project
## Summary
You have **4 microservices** that all work together: 1. **InventoryService** (port 8080) - Manages inventory/tickets 2. **BookingService** (port 8081) - Handles bookings 3. **OrderService** (port 8082) - Manages orders 4. **ApiGateway** (port 8090) - Routes requests to services
## Database Requirement: YES, MANDATORY ⚠️
Your project **CANNOT run without databases**. All services require: - **MySQL** (ticketing database) - Required by InventoryService, BookingService, OrderService - **Kafka** - Required by BookingService and OrderService for messaging - **Keycloak** (with MySQL) - Required by ApiGateway for security
## Option 1: Run with Docker Compose (RECOMMENDED)
This starts all infrastructure (MySQL, Kafka, Keycloak, Prometheus, Grafana):
```bash docker-compose up -d ```
Wait 30-60 seconds for all services to start, then run your Spring Boot applications from IntelliJ.
### What Docker Compose Starts: - MySQL on port 3306 (for your services) - Kafka on port 9092 - Zookeeper on port 2181 - Keycloak on port 8091 - Kafka UI on port 8084 - Prometheus on port 9090 - Grafana on port 3000 - Jenkins on port 8081
To stop: `docker-compose down`
## Option 2: Run in IntelliJ IDEA (All Services in One IDE)
### Step 1: Start Infrastructure ```bash docker-compose up -d ```
### Step 2: Run All Applications in IntelliJ
You can run all 4 services in ONE IntelliJ IDE window:
1. **Open the project** - Open the root folder `microservice3` in IntelliJ 2. **Find all main classes** in the Project view: - `inventoryservice/src/main/java/com/kevinll/inventoryservice/InventoryserviceApplication.java` - `bookingservice/src/main/java/com/kevinll/bookingservice/BookingserviceApplication.java` - `orderservice/src/main/java/com/kevinll/orderservice/OrderserviceApplication.java` - `apigateway/src/main/java/com/kevinll/apigateway/ApigatewayApplication.java`
3. **Run each Application**: - Right-click on each `*Application.java` file - Select "Run '*Application.main()'" - IntelliJ will show all 4 running services in the "Run" panel
4. **Switch between services**: Use the dropdown in the Run panel to see logs for each service
### Why You Can Run All in One IDE: IntelliJ recognizes this as a **multi-module Maven project**. Each service is a separate module, so you can run all 4 applications simultaneously without conflicts.
## Startup Order (IMPORTANT)
1. **First**: `docker-compose up -d` (wait 30-60 seconds) 2. **Second**: InventoryService (port 8080) 3. **Third**: BookingService (port 8081) 4. **Fourth**: OrderService (port 8082) 5. **Last**: ApiGateway (port 8090)
## Verify Everything is Running
- Inventory Service: http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui.html - Booking Service: http://localhost:8081/swagger-ui.html - Order Service: http://localhost:8082 (check logs) - API Gateway: http://localhost:8090/swagger-ui.html - Kafka UI: http://localhost:8084 - Keycloak: http://localhost:8091 (admin/admin) - Prometheus: http://localhost:9090 - Grafana: http://localhost:3000 (admin/admin)
## Troubleshooting
### ⚠️ Port 3306 already in use - MySQL conflict **Error:** `Ports are not available: exposing port TCP 0.0.0.0:3306`
**Problem:** You have MySQL already running on your machine (not in Docker).
**Solutions (choose ONE):**
#### Solution 1: Stop your local MySQL (Recommended if using Docker) ```bash # Stop MySQL Windows service net stop MySQL80
# Or find and stop MySQL in Windows Services (services.msc) ```
Then run: `docker compose up -d`
#### Solution 2: Use your existing MySQL (Skip Docker MySQL) If you want to use your existing MySQL installation:
1. **Don't start MySQL in Docker** - Comment it out in docker-compose.yml: ```yaml # Comment out or remove the mysql service section # mysql: # image: mysql:8.0.39 # ... ```
2. **Make sure your local MySQL has the ticketing database:** - Open MySQL Workbench or command line - Run: `CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS ticketing;` - Your services are configured to use: `root` / `1ktl2710zlA@`
3. **Start only the other services:** ```bash docker compose up -d zookeeper kafka-broker kafka-ui kafka-schema-registry keycloak-db keycloak prometheus grafana ```
#### Solution 3: Change Docker MySQL port If you need BOTH MySQL instances, change the Docker MySQL port:
In `docker-compose.yml`, change: ```yaml mysql: ports: - "3307:3306" # Changed from 3306:3306 ```
Then update ALL service `application.properties` files to use `localhost:3307` instead of `localhost:3306`.
### "docker-compose up -d" not working? ✅ **FIXED!** I corrected syntax errors in your docker-compose.yml: - Fixed Jenkins service configuration - Added missing MySQL root password - Added missing Keycloak MySQL password
### Service won't start - "Connection refused" to MySQL? - Make sure docker-compose is running: `docker ps` - Wait 30-60 seconds after starting docker-compose - Check MySQL is ready: `docker logs mysql`
### Can't find other Application classes in IntelliJ? - Make sure you opened the ROOT folder (`microservice3`), not a sub-folder - IntelliJ should detect the Maven structure automatically - Look in Project view → expand each service folder → src → main → java → com → kevinll
### Port conflicts? If ports 8080-8082, 8090, 3306, 9092, 8091 are taken, you'll need to: - Stop other applications using those ports - Or modify the ports in application.properties files
## Configuration Files
### Passwords Used: - MySQL (ticketing): root / 1ktl2710zlA@ - Keycloak Admin: admin / admin - Keycloak DB: keycloak / password - Grafana: admin / admin
### Service Ports: - InventoryService: 8080 - BookingService: 8081 (⚠️ conflicts with Jenkins in docker-compose) - OrderService: 8082 - ApiGateway: 8090 - MySQL: 3306 - Kafka: 9092 - Keycloak: 8091
## Quick Commands
```bash # Start all infrastructure docker-compose up -d
# Check what's running docker ps
# View logs docker logs mysql docker logs kafka-broker docker logs keycloak
# Stop everything docker-compose down
# Stop and remove volumes (fresh start) docker-compose down -v ```
## Note About Jenkins Port Conflict
⚠️ **Port 8081 is used by BOTH:** - BookingService (Spring Boot) - Jenkins (Docker)
**Solution**: Don't run both at the same time, OR change one of the ports in the respective configuration file.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# ✅ FINAL STEP - Run This Before Pushing to GitHub
## Option 1: Run the cleanup script
Open terminal in your project folder and run:
**Windows (cmd or PowerShell):** ```cmd cd "C:\Projects\Personal Java Projects\microservice3" untrack-sensitive.bat ```
**Mac/Linux:** ```bash chmod +x untrack-sensitive.sh ./untrack-sensitive.sh ```
## Option 2: Run commands manually (if script doesn't work)
Copy and paste these commands one by one:
```cmd cd "C:\Projects\Personal Java Projects\microservice3"
git rm --cached docker-compose.yml git rm --cached inventoryservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached bookingservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached orderservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached apigateway\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached terraform.tfstate git rm -r --cached volume-data
del docker-compose.example.yml del SETUP-CREDENTIALS.md del PRE-PUSH-CHECKLIST.md del SECURITY-REVIEW.md ```
## Then commit and push: ```bash git status git add . git commit -m "Security: Untrack sensitive files and cleanup" git push origin main ```
## ✅ You're Done!
Your sensitive files (passwords, credentials) will NOT be pushed to GitHub.
See `SECURITY-SETUP.md` for more details.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
spring.application.name=apigateway server.port=8090
#OpenAPI springdoc.swagger-ui.path=/swagger-ui.html springdoc.api-docs.path=/v3/api-docs springdoc.swagger-ui.urls[0].name=Inventory Service springdoc.swagger-ui.urls[0].url=/docs/inventoryservice/v3/api-docs springdoc.swagger-ui.urls[1].name=Booking Service springdoc.swagger-ui.urls[1].url=/docs/bookingservice/v3/api-docs
security.excluded.urls= /swagger-ui.html, /swagger-ui/**, /docs/**, /v3/api-docs/**, /swagger-resources/**, /api-docs/**
#Keycloak (configure after Keycloak is set up) #keycloak.auth.jwk-set-uri= http://localhost:8091/realms/ticketing-security-realm/protocol/openid-connect/certs #spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.issuer-uri=http://localhost:8091/realms/ticketing-security-realm
# Actuator management.health.circuitbreakers.enabled=true
#expose all endpoints management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,prometheus management.endpoint.health.show-details=always management.endpoint.prometheus.enabled=true management.metrics.export.prometheus.enabled=true

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
services: mysql: image: mysql:8.0.39 container_name: mysql restart: always environment: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: your_secure_password_here MYSQL_DATABASE: ticketing ports: - "3306:3306" volumes: - ./docker/mysql/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql - db-data:/var/lib/mysql - db-data:/var/log/mysql
zookeeper: image: confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.5.0 hostname: zookeeper container_name: zookeeper ports: - "2181:2181" environment: ZOOKEEPER_CLIENT_PORT: 2181 ZOOKEEPER_TICK_TIME: 3000
kafka-broker: image: confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0 container_name: kafka-broker ports: - "9092:9092" - "29092:29092" depends_on: - zookeeper environment: KAFKA_BROKER_ID: 1 KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT: 'zookeeper:2181' KAFKA_LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP: PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT,PLAINTEXT_HOST:PLAINTEXT KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS: PLAINTEXT://kafka-broker:29092,PLAINTEXT_HOST://localhost:9092 KAFKA_OFFSETS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR: 1 auto.create.topics.enable: 'true'
kafka-ui: container_name: kafka-ui image: provectuslabs/kafka-ui:latest ports: - "8084:8080" depends_on: - kafka-broker environment: KAFKA_CLUSTERS_NAME: local KAFKA_CLUSTERS_BOOTSTRAPSERVERS: kafka-broker:29092 DYNAMIC_CONFIG_ENABLED: 'true'
kafka-schema-registry: image: confluentinc/cp-schema-registry:7.5.0 hostname: kafka-schema-registry container_name: kafka-schema-registry depends_on: - kafka-broker ports: - "8083:8081" environment: SCHEMA_REGISTRY_HOST_NAME: kafka-schema-registry SCHEMA_REGISTRY_KAFKASTORE_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS: 'kafka-broker:29092' SCHEMA_REGISTRY_LISTENERS: http://kafka-schema-registry:8081
keycloak-db: container_name: keycloak-mysql image: mysql:8.3.0 volumes: - ./volume-data/mysql_keycloak_data:/var/lib/mysql environment: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: your_keycloak_root_password MYSQL_DATABASE: keycloak MYSQL_USER: keycloak MYSQL_PASSWORD: your_keycloak_password
keycloak: container_name: keycloak image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:24.0.1 command: [ "start-dev", "--import-realm" ] environment: DB_VENDOR: MYSQL DB_ADDR: mysql DB_DATABASE: keycloak DB_USER: keycloak DB_PASSWORD: your_keycloak_password KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: your_admin_password ports: - "8091:8080" volumes: - ./docker/keycloak/realms/:/opt/keycloak/data/import/ depends_on: - keycloak-db
jenkins: container_name: jenkins build: context: . dockerfile: jenkins.Dockerfile restart: always ports: - "8081:8080" - "50000:50000" volumes: - jenkins-data:/var/jenkins_home - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock - ./:/workspace environment: - DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock command: > bash -c " apt-get update && apt-get install -y docker.io awscli maven && usermod -aG docker jenkins && /usr/local/bin/jenkins.sh "
prometheus: image: prom/prometheus:latest container_name: prometheus ports: - "9090:9090" volumes: - ./docker/prometheus/prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml - prometheus-data:/prometheus command: - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml' - '--storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus' restart: unless-stopped
grafana: image: grafana/grafana:latest container_name: grafana ports: - "3000:3000" environment: - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_USER=admin - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=your_grafana_password volumes: - grafana-data:/var/lib/grafana depends_on: - prometheus restart: unless-stopped
volumes: db-data: jenkins-data: prometheus-data: grafana-data:

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
@echo off REM Script to remove sensitive files from Git tracking REM Run this BEFORE pushing to GitHub
echo Removing sensitive files from Git tracking... echo.
REM Remove application.properties files from Git git rm --cached inventoryservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached bookingservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached orderservice\src\main\resources\application.properties git rm --cached apigateway\src\main\resources\application.properties
REM Remove docker-compose.yml from Git git rm --cached docker-compose.yml
REM Remove terraform state files from Git git rm --cached terraform.tfstate git rm --cached terraform\terraform.tfstate git rm --cached terraform\terraform.tfstate.backup
REM Remove volume-data directory git rm -r --cached volume-data\
echo. echo Done! Sensitive files have been removed from Git tracking. echo. echo NEXT STEPS: echo 1. Commit these changes: git commit -m "Remove sensitive files from tracking" echo 2. Verify with: git status echo 3. Then push: git push origin main echo. pause

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== Cleaning up extra files ===" rm -f docker-compose.example.yml rm -f SETUP-CREDENTIALS.md rm -f PRE-PUSH-CHECKLIST.md rm -f SECURITY-REVIEW.md rm -f remove-sensitive-files.bat rm -f */src/main/resources/application.properties.example rm -f */*/src/main/resources/application.properties.example
echo "" echo "=== Untracking sensitive files from Git ===" git rm --cached docker-compose.yml 2>/dev/null || echo "docker-compose.yml not tracked" git rm --cached inventoryservice/src/main/resources/application.properties 2>/dev/null || echo "inventoryservice application.properties not tracked" git rm --cached bookingservice/src/main/resources/application.properties 2>/dev/null || echo "bookingservice application.properties not tracked" git rm --cached orderservice/src/main/resources/application.properties 2>/dev/null || echo "orderservice application.properties not tracked" git rm --cached apigateway/src/main/resources/application.properties 2>/dev/null || echo "apigateway application.properties not tracked" git rm --cached terraform.tfstate 2>/dev/null || echo "terraform.tfstate not tracked" git rm --cached terraform/terraform.tfstate 2>/dev/null || echo "terraform/terraform.tfstate not tracked" git rm --cached terraform/terraform.tfstate.backup 2>/dev/null || echo "terraform backup not tracked" git rm -r --cached volume-data/ 2>/dev/null || echo "volume-data not tracked"
echo "" echo "=== Done! ===" echo "" echo "Next steps:" echo "1. Check status: git status" echo "2. Commit changes: git add . && git commit -m 'Security: Untrack sensitive files'" echo "3. Push: git push origin main"

option.name: updatedContent
